agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Sequential multi-step mutation with zero error handling leaves orphaned state on any partial failure"
  },
  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "Orphaned courier reservation on downstream failure",
      "risk": "If Step 2 (updateStatus) or Step 3/4 (notifications) throw, the courier remains reserved but the order is never assigned or the courier never notified. The courier is locked out of future orders with no cleanup path.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "assignAndDispatch — between Step 1 and Steps 2-4",
      "reproduction": "1. Call assignAndDispatch('order-1'). 2. courierService.reserve succeeds, returning courier-A. 3. orderService.updateStatus throws (e.g., DB timeout). 4. courier-A is now reserved indefinitely — no rollback releases them. Under load, couriers are drained from the pool with no recovery.",
      "fix": "Wrap Steps 2-4 in a try/catch that calls courierService.release(courierId) on failure. Alternatively, use a saga/compensation pattern: record each completed step and roll back in reverse order on any failure.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Courier availability, order assignment reliability"
    },
    {
      "title": "Order marked ASSIGNED but courier never dispatched",
      "risk": "If Step 2 succeeds but Step 3 (sendDispatchAlert) fails, the order is in ASSIGNED state with a courier who was never told to go. The customer may also be notified (Step 4 never runs) or not — either way the order is stuck with no retry or reconciliation.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "assignAndDispatch — between Step 2 and Step 3",
      "reproduction": "1. Reserve succeeds. 2. updateStatus succeeds (order is now ASSIGNED). 3. sendDispatchAlert throws (push service down). 4. Order sits in ASSIGNED forever — courier doesn't know, customer doesn't know, no retry fires.",
      "fix": "Either: (a) make notifications retriable with a dead-letter queue so transient failures don't corrupt order state, or (b) only transition to ASSIGNED after dispatch confirmation, using an intermediate ASSIGNING state that a background reconciler can sweep.",
      "time_to_fix": "1-2 hours",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Order fulfillment SLA, courier dispatch reliability"
    },
    {
      "title": "No idempotency — retries cause double-reservation",
      "risk": "If a caller retries assignAndDispatch after a transient failure (e.g., network blip after Step 1), courierService.reserve is called again for the same orderId, potentially reserving a second courier while the first remains locked.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "assignAndDispatch — Step 1 entry point",
      "reproduction": "1. Call assignAndDispatch('order-1'). 2. Reserve succeeds (courier-A locked). 3. Timeout occurs before updateStatus response reaches caller. 4. Caller retries. 5. Reserve called again — courier-B now also locked for order-1. Two couriers reserved, at most one dispatched.",
      "fix": "Make reserve idempotent by keying on orderId (return existing reservation if one exists), or add an idempotency key / check-before-reserve guard at the top of assignAndDispatch that short-circuits if the order already has a courier.",
      "time_to_fix": "1 hour",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Courier pool integrity, order consistency"
    }
  ],
  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No timeout or circuit breaker on external calls",
      "description": "Four sequential awaits with no timeout means a single slow service (e.g., notification provider) blocks the entire flow indefinitely. Under degraded conditions this will exhaust connection pools or serverless concurrency.",
      "location": "assignAndDispatch — all await calls"
    },
    {
      "title": "Customer notification failure is silent data loss",
      "description": "Step 4 failure means the customer is never told their order is on the way, but the system considers the dispatch complete. At minimum, Step 4 should be fire-and-forget with a retry queue rather than a hard failure that could also roll back a successful dispatch.",
      "location": "assignAndDispatch — Step 4"
    }
  ],
  "suggestions": [
    "Separate the critical path (reserve + assign) from side-effects (notifications) — dispatch notifications via an event/queue so notification failures don't block or corrupt order state.",
    "Add structured logging at each step boundary so partial failures are diagnosable in production.",
    "Consider a state machine for order status transitions to prevent illegal states and enable background reconciliation."
  ],
  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "Any partial failure leaves permanently orphaned state (locked couriers, stuck orders) with no self-healing mechanism. Under real traffic, this compounds quickly.",
    "rollback_capability": "No rollback logic exists. Rolling back the deployment doesn't fix already-corrupted state in the database.",
    "backward_compatibility": "N/A — new function, no backward compat concern.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No logging, no metrics, no alerting. Orphaned couriers and stuck orders will be invisible until customers complain.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": false,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": []
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Cannot safely deploy. Fix error handling and compensation logic before shipping."
  },
  "escalations": [],
  "confidence": {
    "level": 95,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Orphaned reservation on partial failure — deterministic from code structure",
      "Lack of idempotency — no guard visible",
      "Stuck ASSIGNED state — sequential dependency chain with no compensation"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether courierService.reserve is already idempotent internally — cannot verify without service implementation"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "courierService.reserve locks a courier and has no automatic expiry or internal idempotency",
      "No external retry/saga orchestrator wraps this function"
    ]
  }
}

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this code for critical issues.

The function has a sequential multi-step process with no error handling, no rollback, and no idempotency. Let me identify the top risks.
